evals.prompt: Review my current branch. It changes 2 source files with 74 added/deleted lines. No public/shared behavior, API, schema, auth, dependency, async lifecycle, state, or config is touched.
evals.expected_output: After passing runtime/session preflight, the review selects Tiny from production files only, runs deterministic relevant tests, and writes verified v3 report/sidecar artifacts.
expectations: The profile is Tiny only because scopeStatus is pass, production-only filesChanged <= 3, changedLines <= 100, and riskTriggers is empty; the classifier has no docsOnly field
Exactly one dedicated Build Validator runs per repo with the active tool's fast runtime profile
Requirement and specialist agents are skipped while the main agent reviews requirement, regression, security, performance, design, standards, and tests
Trusted preflight records exact modelId and effort; report Main Runtime equals the attestation rather than settings, self-report, or not exposed
The report contains exact Skill, Version, Runtime, Triggered, and Skipped fields and the sidecar contains recordVersion 3 with hash-bound runtime, scope, and test artifacts
Branch Work Item Gate runs with the Build Validator runtime for branch/PR scope and records PASS/WARN/FAIL/SKIPPED in the report and sidecar

evals.prompt: Review a 1-file, 35-line change that modifies a public API DTO and authorization behavior. No linked work item is available.
evals.expected_output: The small diff is classified Pro because API and auth risks forbid Tiny. Build and regression-only Requirement validators run, Security and relevant design specialists trigger, and unrelated behavior preservation is evidenced.
expectations: The classifier selects Pro despite the Tiny size thresholds because api-contract and auth-security-boundary triggers are announced
Requirement Validator always runs in regression-only mode with the active tool's deep runtime profile
Security Reviewer runs through the generic code-reviewer standard runtime while untriggered specialists are explicitly skipped
Requirement evidence compares base/new behavior and cites symbols, callers or consumers, events/state where relevant, tests, and unrelated behavior preservation
A malformed or type-mismatched branch/work-item gate failure is reported as CRITICAL and stops Requirement Validator and specialists

evals.prompt: Review Azure DevOps PR 75635 and follow its linked direct work item. Treat the parent only as context. Verify changed symbols, callers/consumers, events/state, tests, and preserved behavior; then prepare a publish-compatible report.
evals.expected_output: PR 75635 receives a Pro review with direct-AC mapping, parent-context separation, risk-triggered specialist routing, stable report metadata, ADO guard, and strict v3 follow-up records.
expectations: The review announces Pro profile plus every classifier and specialist trigger before dispatch
The direct work item's acceptance criteria are binding and parent context does not create extra Missing or Partial criteria
Build Validator and Requirement Validator always run; only specialists with recorded evidence triggers run after a passing build

The report passes the ADO autolink guard and scripts/verify_output.py using its generated runtime attestation, and its sidecar includes skillName code-review-pro, skillVersion 3.0.0, and reviewProfile
A later follow-up reclassifies only the delta with the same No-production-code/Tiny/Pro classifier and writes recordVersion 3
The PR source branch ID is verified through az boards work-item show, the ADO work item type must be User Story, Bug, or Issue, and branch prefix mismatch is WARN not FAIL

v3_contract_cases: Blocked or confirmation-required runtime stops before repository reads; an existing session proceeds only with a recorded override.
Tests/docs-only scope becomes No-production-code with no worktree, tests, semantic agents, or findings.
A production finding may cite test evidence; a test/doc/generated/vendor/binary finding is invalid.
Missing direct tests produce exactly the use-unit-testing advisory; executions[] supports multiple repositories and routes fail/timeout results to a blocking test gate.
The verifier rejects pre-v3 records and validates retained branch, actor, child-runtime, PR-only, merge-preview, dependency, and follow-up provenance without an expected-runtime argument.
The report contains Runtime Evidence, Scope Evidence, Test Evidence, and Semantic Review sections, and report finding targets exactly match production-only sidecar findings.
non_goals: Do not sync, install, publish, or modify deployed copies of the skill
Do not use file/line thresholds alone to classify risky changes as Tiny
Do not spawn all specialists by default or skip the Pro Requirement Validator when no work item exists
Do not treat parent Feature or Epic outcomes as direct acceptance criteria
Do not create worktrees outside the reviewed repository
